statement: AMD's MI300X offers the most raw FP8 compute per hardware dollar — about 2,615 TFLOPS for $10,000-$15,000, roughly 2.1e11 FLOPS per dollar — versus NVIDIA's B200 at 4,500 TFLOPS for $30,000-$40,000, an on-paper cost-per-FLOP edge of roughly 1.6x for AMD.
why: TFLOPS per purchase dollar is the raw acquisition-cost efficiency underpinning AMD's 30-50% cheaper hardware pitch.

impact.mechanism: On paper AMD undercuts NVIDIA on dollars-per-FLOP, pressuring merchant-GPU pricing, but the source notes software and utilization overhead erase much of it for training.

evidence.source: Silicon Analysts — NVIDIA vs AMD AI GPU Specifications & Pricing (April 2026)
Silicon Analysts — NVIDIA vs AMD AI GPU Specifications & Pricing (April 2026)
evidence.excerpt: TCO: AMD systems '30-50% cheaper' in hardware, though 'software utilization and engineering overhead erase much of it for training.'

confidence.basis: Secondary source; ratio derived from stated TFLOPS and broad price ranges.

dispersion: conflicting same-key reports: statement/trend changed (trend unknown -> unknown); sources: GPUAdvisor — NVIDIA B300 Ultra vs AMD MI355X (March 2026), Silicon Analysts — NVIDIA vs AMD AI GPU Specifications & Pricing (April 2026)
